name: gsap
summary: The professional-grade JavaScript animation library — animate anything (DOM, CSS, SVG, Canvas, WebGL) with a timeline-based API.

story.problem: CSS animations struggle with complex sequences, can't animate JavaScript values or WebGL uniforms, and have no scroll synchronization. framer-motion is React-specific. You need animations that work everywhere with precise timing control.
story.mental_model: GSAP's core primitive is a tween: gsap.to(target, { duration, properties }). Timelines sequence tweens: const tl = gsap.timeline(); tl.to(...).to(...). ScrollTrigger pins elements and scrubs timelines to scroll position. GSAP tweens anything with a numeric property — DOM, three.js objects, plain objects.
story.when_to_use: Marketing landing pages, scroll-driven storytelling, complex animation sequences, SVG/logo animations, parallax effects, or any animation that needs to be framework-agnostic.
story.when_not_to_use: Simple React UI micro-interactions where framer-motion or CSS is simpler. GSAP's free tier doesn't include ScrollSmoother and some plugins commercially.

alternatives.name: anime.js
alternatives.reason: Lightweight free alternative for simpler cases

nodes.label: gsap.to()
nodes.summary: Tween from current state to target values

nodes.label: gsap.from()
nodes.summary: Tween from specified values to current state

nodes.label: gsap.timeline()
nodes.summary: Sequence multiple tweens with relative timing

nodes.label: stagger
nodes.summary: Delay each target in a group by an offset

nodes.label: ScrollTrigger
nodes.summary: Link animations to scroll position — plugin
